data.narrative:
Terrestrial Energy operates in the nuclear energy sector as a public company, having raised $280M to date. The comparable universe consists of three public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and five private peers including Oklo, Nano Nuclear Energy, X-Energy, Fuse Energy, and NuScale Power—eight firms total across the sector.

Peer-median methodology is not applicable to this valuation. The nuclear energy sector contains only four same-stage peers overall, and filtering by stage, adjacency, or valuation tier yields fewer than three datapoints in any subset. A peer-median approach would require at least three comparable transactions to be defensible; the narrow peer set does not support this threshold. Applying an all-sector median would introduce substantial distortion given the heterogeneity in business model, technology maturity, and capital intensity across the nuclear energy universe.

Without triangulation across multiple quantitative methods, a specific valuation range cannot be derived from the data provided. The primary uncertainty stems from the scarcity of same-stage comparables with sufficient similarity to Terrestrial Energy's technology, development phase, and cost structure. Any valuation conclusion would depend on either (1) alternative methodologies not present in this dataset, such as DCF or revenue multiples applied to forward projections, or (2) expansion of the peer set to include broader energy infrastructure or advanced manufacturing comparables at the cost of sector specificity.
